ogTitle: North Boyanup vs North Cascade: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Boyanup and North Cascade.
heading: North Boyanup vs North Cascade — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Boyanup, Western Australia and North Cascade, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Boyanup has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to North Cascade (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Boyanup or North Cascade safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Boyanup has a flood risk score of 44/100, while North Cascade scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Boyanup scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. North Cascade scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Boyanup or North Cascade?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Boyanup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
